codeReview.summary: Core URL-param routing was implemented, but a blocking navigation-history bug remains: the router uses replace for all view/date transitions, so history entries are overwritten and browser-back context preservation (mapped assertion) is not satisfied.

issues.description: View/date navigation uses `router.replace(...)` for every state transition (also at line 90). This overwrites history entries instead of pushing them, so users cannot reliably return to the prior in-dashboard context via browser Back. This conflicts with the feature's mapped assertion VAL-CROSS-009 (context preserved on browser back).

Query reconstruction keeps only `view` and `date` (`buildSearchString`), and the recovery render path does not read/forward `event_id` (line 129). Deep links like `/dashboard?view=recovery&date=...&event_id=...` lose triggering-event context that the dedicated recovery page currently supports.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The workflow guidance did not prevent a mismatch between expected browser-back behavior and implementation choice (`replace`). The worker explicitly treated the replace/history behavior as desirable, despite this feature mapping to VAL-CROSS-009 (back-context preservation).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: validation-contract.md:464-467 defines VAL-CROSS-009; features.json:1094-1098 maps this feature to VAL-CROSS-009; worker-transcripts.jsonl:40 records the worker rationale that replace-based history behavior is desired.

summary: Review failed: routing/deep-link basics are present, but browser history behavior is incompatible with the feature's mapped back-navigation assertion. Report written with one blocking and one non-blocking issue.
